tldr: Primer is a full payments orchestration platform with checkout, workflows and PSP routing, while Basis Theory is a processor-agnostic tokenization vault. Moving means keeping the vault and rebuilding the routing and workflow layer yourself.
why_switch: Want to own routing and orchestration logic in-house
Need a portable card vault not tied to a PSP
Reduce PCI scope without orchestration lock-in
Optimize for vault portability across PSPs
export_method: From Primer, export workflows, connector configurations, customer records and transaction history through the dashboard and API. For card tokens, request a PCI-compliant token export from Primer support to Basis Theory — this is a regulated, processor-to-processor transfer and cannot be done over a public API. Document each workflow as a flowchart so you can rebuild equivalent routing logic on top of Basis Theory. Pull webhook configurations and any 3DS/SCA settings for reference, and keep at least 12-24 months of transaction history in your warehouse.
import_method: Basis Theory provides the vault, not orchestration. Onboard with Basis Theory by setting up a tenant, defining token schemas, and integrating their SDK in your checkout. For existing tokens, coordinate the PCI bulk import with Basis Theory support so cards arrive vaulted on their side. Then build a routing layer in your own service: call your PSPs directly using Basis Theory tokens via their proxy, and re-implement retries, fallbacks, 3DS and reconciliation. Move connectors over one PSP at a time, run Primer in parallel, and cut traffic to the new path gradually behind a feature flag.
effort: 6-12 weeks, mostly to rebuild orchestration logic
gotchas: Basis Theory replaces the vault, not the workflow engine — you own routing now
PCI token migration is a manual, regulated process with weeks of lead time
Each PSP integration that Primer abstracted must be re-implemented
3DS and SCA flows often need rework because they were handled in Primer
Reconciliation logic moves into your stack and needs careful testing
Run both in parallel — do not cut over without traffic shadowing

email.subject: Moving from Primer to Basis Theory in 14 days
email.body:
Hi team,

Over the next 14 days we are migrating our payments setup from Primer to Basis Theory. The reason is that we want a portable, processor-agnostic vault and to own the routing and workflow logic in our own services rather than in a third-party orchestrator.

The rough plan: stand up a Basis Theory tenant, request a PCI-compliant token transfer from Primer, and rebuild our PSP routing as an internal service that calls the existing PSPs through Basis Theory's proxy. We will keep Primer live, mirror traffic to the new path behind a feature flag, then shift PSPs over one by one.

A few callouts. 3DS, retries and fallback logic that lived in Primer workflows need to be rebuilt. Webhook consumers will change. Reconciliation will need extra eyes during the cutover. Card data is moving via a regulated PCI process — please do not try to shortcut this.

Let me know if you own anything touching Primer directly.

Thanks,
Your customer
